name: RABBIT-CAGE-18
title: Workflow scripts assume bash — break on tcsh: xargs quoting failure
description: When Claude runs ad-hoc backlog/bug listing commands on a tcsh host, the xargs -I{} approach fails because the shell interprets Python parentheses as shell subshell syntax before Python sees them. Error: 'sh: command substitution: line 1: syntax error near unexpected token "("'. Root cause: scripts and contracts do not account for tcsh as the user shell. Requirement: all workflow scripts and contracts must support both bash and csh/tcsh.

history.note: Root cause was Claude generating ad-hoc find|xargs pipelines in tcsh because list-bugs.sh lacked severity in --text output and list-backlog.sh did not exist. Both gaps are now closed: list-bugs.sh --text includes severity, list-backlog.sh added with priority and --text support. All workflow scripts use explicit #!/bin/bash shebangs and are shell-agnostic at the OS level. No script defect remains.
